section_name: Grounds for Refusal to Issue or for Revocation or Suspension of Title

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A title may be refused, canceled, suspended, or revoked by the department if:
the application contains a false or fraudulent statement;
the applicant failed to furnish required information requested by the department;
the applicant is not entitled to a title;
the department has reason to believe that the motor vehicle is stolen;
the department has reason to believe that the issuance of a title would defraud the owner or a lienholder of the motor vehicle;
the registration for the motor vehicle is suspended or revoked; or
the required fee has not been paid.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The department may rescind, cancel, or revoke an application for a title if a notarized or county-stamped affidavit is presented containing:
a statement that the vehicle involved was a new motor vehicle in the process of a first sale;
a statement that the dealer, the applicant, and any lienholder have canceled the sale;

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: was never in the possession of the title applicant; or

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: was in the possession of the title applicant; and
the signatures of the dealer, the applicant, and any lienholder.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: A rescission, cancellation, or revocation containing the statement authorized under Subsection (b)(3)(B) does not negate the fact that the vehicle has been the subject of a previous retail sale.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The department shall place a hold on processing a title application for a motor vehicle if the department receives a request for a hold accompanied by evidence of a legal action regarding ownership of or a lien interest in the motor vehicle. The hold shall continue until a final, nonappealable judgment is entered in the action or the party requesting the hold requests that the hold be removed.

history.raw: Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 959 (S.B.1817), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2021.
